rows.label: Between
rows.english: Between the pharmacy and the post office.

scenarios.situation: You call 155 (the Emergency lesson) and need to tell the dispatcher you're standing outside the building. Which word do you use?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Vonku = Outside — critical when calling emergency services and giving your position.

scenarios.situation: Your friend texts: 'Čakám pred nemocnicou.' Where are they waiting?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Pred = in front of — Nemocnica = hospital (the Emergency lesson). Pred nemocnicou = in front of the hospital.

scenarios.situation: You want to meet someone at the café directly across from the bank. How do you say 'opposite the bank'?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Naproti = opposite / across from — one of the most useful meeting-point words in Slovak.

scenarios.situation: At the supermarket checkout, you remember your card is in the car outside. What do you say?
scenarios.answerMeaning: The card is outside. — vonku = outside (this lesson) + karta = card (the I Don't Understand lesson). Two lessons in one sentence!

scenarios.situation: The police need your exact location. You're standing between the pharmacy and the post office. What do you say? (the Emergency lesson)
scenarios.answerMeaning: I am between the pharmacy and the post office. — medzi = between. Perfect for the Emergency lesson's call script (Volám sa... Som na...)!

scenarios.situation: The shopping centre map shows 'Lekáreň: nad reštauráciou.' Where is the pharmacy?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Nad = above / over. Reštaurácia = restaurant. Nad reštauráciou = above the restaurant.

scenarios.situation: Your colleague says the storage room is 'pod schodmi, vľavo.' Where should you look?
choices: To the right of the stairs
scenarios.answerMeaning: Pod = below / under. Schody = stairs. Pod schodmi = under the stairs — very common in Slovak buildings!

scenarios.situation: You call 155 (the Emergency lesson) and need to give a precise landmark. You can see the pharmacy right next to you. What do you tell the dispatcher?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Pri = at / right next to. Som pri lekárni = I am right at the pharmacy — more precise than pred (in front of) when pinpointing your location for emergency services!

scenarios.situation: You walked past the café entrance and are now on the other side. Your friend calls asking where you are. What do you say?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Za = behind / past. Som za kaviarňou = I'm behind/past the café. Easy to overshoot your stop!

items.translation: The police station is ___ the hospital.
We're meeting ___ the hospital at three.

items.sentence: Som ___ lekárne a pošty. Ste na správnom mieste!
items.translation: I'm ___ the pharmacy and the post office. You're in the right place!

items.sentence: Kaviareň je ___ banky. Vidíte ju cez ulicu.
items.translation: The café is ___ the bank. You can see it across the street.

items.sentence: Karta je ___. Idem ju zobrať z auta.
items.translation: The card is ___. I'm going to get it from the car.
